title: 谋杀启事
summary:
Miss Marple and Yuan Yuan discuss various criminal archetypes and past cases from St. Mary Mead to analyze the murder of Letitia Blacklock. Miss Marple recounts several examples of deception and motive, including a nurse who administered morphine to elderly patients for their inheritance, a young man who framed a woman for his thefts, and a woman whose husband was a forger. They also discuss Joan Crowfoot, who once disarmed a bank robber.

Yuan Yuan suggests that the suspects Pip and Emma might be responsible, noting that if they are, the culprits could be Patrick and Julia. Miss Marple considers various possibilities, including the involvement of Pip or Emma's spouses, their mother, or a father who might be masquerading as a British army officer. Miss Marple emphasizes that people commit terrible crimes for large sums of money, often failing to consider the repercussions. Yuan Yuan reflects on how one might justify murder by pretending the money will be used for charitable purposes, such as helping abandoned children, but concludes she could never bring herself to kill anyone.

The conversation is briefly interrupted by a waitress named Julia, who reacts to being discussed before taking a tip and leaving. Miss Marple expresses urgency, noting that the Scottish old lady could die at any moment. The narrative then shifts to Inspector Cordiner, who arrives at a small station in the Scottish Highlands. He reflects on the isolation of the wealthy, disabled Mrs. Goddard, wondering if she chose to live in such a remote location due to her illness or a desire to cut herself off from society.
